title: 1929 vs 2025: Andrew Ross Sorkin on Crashes, Bubbles & Lessons Learned
description:
(0:00) Chamath and Friedberg welcome Andrew Ross Sorkin to discuss his new book, "1929: Inside the Greatest Crash in Wall Street History--and How It Shattered a Nation"

(0:38) Why he chose this time period

(3:22) The setup: what led to the 1929 crash

(19:24) The characters: major players in the 1929 crash; what kind of bubble are we experiencing in 2025?

(26:21) Role of journalist vs market participant; characters of the 2025 market

(30:10) AI's potential 1929-like impact on unemployment

(35:16) Why socialism is flaring up now more than it did post-1929

(40:34) Does the US need a 2025 "New Deal" on cutting spending, tariff balancing act

(46:51) Film rights strategy

analysis.summary: Andrew Ross Sorkin discusses his new book, '1929: Inside the Greatest Crash in Wall Street History--and How It Shattered a Nation', and draws parallels between the 1929 crash and the current market in 2025. The conversation covers the setup and characters of the 1929 crash, the role of journalists and market participants, and the potential impact of AI on unemployment. Sorkin also shares his thoughts on the need for a 'New Deal' in 2025 and the resurgence of socialism.

takeaways.text: The 1929 crash was caused by a combination of factors, including overproduction and underconsumption
The current market in 2025 is experiencing a bubble, but its nature is different from the 1929 bubble
AI has the potential to cause significant unemployment, similar to the 1929 crash
Socialism is flaring up in 2025 due to income inequality and economic uncertainty
A 'New Deal' may be necessary in 2025 to address economic issues, including cutting spending and tariff balancing
Journalists play a crucial role in holding market participants accountable and providing context
The characters of the 2025 market are different from those of the 1929 market
The film rights strategy for Sorkin's book is an important consideration

quotes.text: History doesn't repeat, but it rhymes
quotes.speaker: Andrew Ross Sorkin

sentiment.notes: The conversation is cautious and reflective, with a focus on the potential risks and challenges of the current market. The tone is serious and thoughtful, with a sense of urgency and concern.

notable_moments.description: Discussion of AI's potential impact on unemployment
Discussion of the resurgence of socialism
Discussion of the need for a 'New Deal' in 2025
